text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, status post right radical nephrectomy, currently on cabozantinib 40 mg daily (dose-reduced from 60 mg) as second-line therapy following progression on ipilimumab/nivolumab. This examination is performed for restaging at approximately 9 months of cabozantinib therapy (18 months from initial diagnosis). Prior comparison study available: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast obtained approximately 3 months prior.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of iohexol 350 intravenous contrast material, with images acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal reformations were obtained. Low-dose technique applied through the chest given cumulative radiation exposure. Images reviewed on standard soft tissue, lung, and bone window settings.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast, prior study (approximately 3 months ago).

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesion identified.

The previously described bilateral pulmonary nodules are again seen, with the following observations:

- Right lower lobe: The dominant nodule in the posterior basal segment measures approximately 1.5 cm in greatest axial dimension, decreased from 1.8 cm on the prior study. The nodule demonstrates soft tissue attenuation without internal cavitation or ground-glass halo. Margins remain well-circumscribed and smooth. This represents modest interval decrease in size.

- Right upper lobe: A soft tissue nodule in the posterior segment measures approximately 0.7 cm, decreased from 0.9 cm previously. No associated cavitation or pleural tethering.

- Left lower lobe: A nodule in the superior segment measures approximately 1.1 cm, essentially unchanged compared with the prior examination (1.1 cm). Smooth margins, soft tissue attenuation. Stable in appearance.

- Left upper lobe: A small nodule in the left upper lobe lingular segment measures approximately 0.3 cm, unchanged. A second tiny nodule in the left upper lobe anterior segment measures less than 3 mm, stable and likely below the threshold of reliable measurement.

- The few scattered additional bilateral pulmonary nodules measuring up to 3-4 mm, first noted on the study obtained approximately 6 months prior to this examination, remain stable without interval growth or new nodule formation. None demonstrate features of primary lung malignancy.

No new pulmonary nodules are identified above the threshold of reliable detection. No lobar or segmental consolidation. No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No mediastinal or hilar adenopathy. The heart is normal in size with no pericardial effusion. A few small prevascular lymph nodes measuring up to 6 mm are stable and below threshold for pathologic enlargement.

Chest Wall and Osseous Thorax: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No rib destruction or pathologic fracture. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, unchanged.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a smooth hepatic contour. Hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal hypervascular or hypovascular lesion. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Biliary System: Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis or cholecystitis. Common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen: Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas: Pancreatic parenchyma enhances normally throughout the head, neck, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collection.

Adrenal Glands: The right adrenal gland is not identified, consistent with prior surgical absence (patient is status post right radical nephrectomy). The left adrenal gland is normal in morphology and attenuation, without evidence of metastatic involvement.

Kidneys and Retroperitoneum: The right nephrectomy bed demonstrates expected postoperative changes with a small amount of chronic-appearing fibrotic soft tissue in the right retroperitoneum, unchanged compared with the prior study. No new right retroperitoneal mass or abscess. The left kidney is normal in size and demonstrates symmetric cortical enhancement. No hydronephrosis of the left collecting system. No left renal mass identified.

The retroperitoneal aortic and para-aortic regions, previously sites of metastatic nodal disease, demonstrate no definite enlarged lymph nodes above the level of the prior surgical resection. The small residual soft tissue stranding in the right para-aortic region, which has been stable over multiple prior studies, again appears similar and most likely represents post-surgical fibrosis rather than viable nodal disease.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Small and large bowel loops are normal in caliber and distribution without wall thickening, obstruction, or mesenteric stranding. No free air or free fluid in the abdomen. Appendix not definitively visualized but no periappendiceal inflammatory changes.

Abdominal Wall and Peritoneum: No peritoneal nodularity or ascites to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis.

---

PELVIS:

Right Pelvic Sidewall Lymphadenopathy: The previously identified right pelvic sidewall lymph node is again seen and measures approximately 1.3 cm in short axis, stable compared with the prior study (1.3 cm). Compared with the initial study at the time of progression from immunotherapy (1.9 cm), this node has substantially decreased in size since initiation of cabozantinib and appears to have reached a plateau. The node demonstrates mildly heterogeneous enhancement without central necrosis.

Right External Iliac Region: The previously described right external iliac lymph node now measures approximately 0.8 cm in short axis, slightly decreased from 1.0 cm on the prior examination. This node appeared on the study demonstrating progression from immunotherapy (measuring 1.4 cm) and has shown interval improvement since initiation of cabozantinib.

No new pelvic lymphadenopathy identified. Left external iliac, obturator, and inguinal nodal chains are within normal limits bilaterally.

Bladder: The urinary bladder is well-distended and smooth-walled. No intraluminal filling defect or wall irregularity. No hydronephrosis in the left ureter.

Gynecologic Structures: The uterus is normal in size with no myometrial mass. Bilateral adnexa are unremarkable. No free pelvic fluid.

Osseous Pelvis: No lytic, sclerotic, or expansile osseous lesion to suggest metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes at the bilateral sacroiliac joints and acetabular regions are unchanged.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Bilateral pulmonary metastases: Modest continued interval decrease in the dominant right lower lobe nodule (now approximately 1.5 cm, previously 1.8 cm) and the right upper lobe nodule (now approximately 0.7 cm, previously 0.9 cm). The left lower lobe nodule remains stable at 1.1 cm. The remaining small bilateral pulmonary nodules are stable without new nodule formation. Overall, the pulmonary disease burden continues to show a slow but sustained favorable trend, most pronounced in the right-sided lesions.

2. Pelvic nodal disease: The right pelvic sidewall lymph node is stable at approximately 1.3 cm compared with the prior study. The right external iliac node has decreased slightly to 0.8 cm. Both nodes remain substantially improved compared with their sizes at the nadir of disease progression on immunotherapy. No new pelvic adenopathy.

3. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or peritoneal involvement.

4. Status post right radical nephrectomy: Expected postoperative changes at the right nephrectomy bed with stable chronic fibrotic change; no evidence of local recurrence.

5. Left kidney appears intact without hydronephrosis.

Overall, the findings are consistent with continued disease control on cabozantinib therapy, with modestly ongoing response in select pulmonary lesions and stability of previously responding nodal disease. The pattern is most consistent with maintained therapeutic benefit from the current regimen.
